quotes.text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
quotes.author: Socrates

quotes.text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
quotes.author: Seneca

quotes.text: You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: No man is free who is not master of himself.
quotes.author: Epictetus

quotes.text: It is not that we have a short time to live, but that we waste a great deal of it.
quotes.author: Seneca

quotes.text: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: He who fears death will never do anything worthy of a man who is alive.
quotes.author: Seneca

quotes.text: First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do.
quotes.author: Epictetus

quotes.text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without.
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: In the end, only three things matter: how much you loved, how gently you lived, and how gracefully you let go of things not meant for you.
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
quotes.author: Steve Jobs

quotes.text: Your task is not to seek for love, but merely to seek and find all the barriers within yourself that you have built against it.
quotes.author: Rumi

quotes.text: The wound is the place where the Light enters you.
quotes.author: Rumi

quotes.text: Yesterday I was clever, so I wanted to change the world. Today I am wise, so I am changing myself.
quotes.author: Rumi

quotes.text: When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: Between stimulus and response there is a space. In that space is our freedom and power to choose our response.
quotes.author: Viktor Frankl

quotes.text: Everything can be taken from a man but one thing: the last of the human freedoms — to choose one's attitude in any given set of circumstances.
quotes.author: Viktor Frankl

quotes.text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
quotes.author: Friedrich Nietzsche

quotes.text: What a man can be, he must be. This need we call self-actualization.
quotes.author: Abraham Maslow

quotes.text: The privilege of a lifetime is to become who you truly are.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: Until you make the unconscious conscious, it will direct your life and you will call it fate.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: Who looks outside, dreams; who looks inside, awakes.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: The curious paradox is that when I accept myself just as I am, then I can change.
quotes.author: Carl Rogers

quotes.text: People are not disturbed by things but by the views they take of them.
quotes.author: Epictetus

quotes.text: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
quotes.author: Will Durant

quotes.text: The only person you are destined to become is the person you decide to be.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: Do not go where the path may lead, go instead where there is no path and leave a trail.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: It is during our darkest moments that we must focus to see the light.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
quotes.author: Nelson Mandela

quotes.text: Life is not about finding yourself. Life is about creating yourself.
quotes.author: George Bernard Shaw

quotes.text: Be the change you wish to see in the world.
quotes.author: Mahatma Gandhi

quotes.text: In three words I can sum up everything I've learned about life: it goes on.
quotes.author: Robert Frost

quotes.text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
quotes.author: Chinese Proverb

quotes.text: Your beliefs become your thoughts, your thoughts become your words, your words become your actions, your actions become your habits, your habits become your values, your values become your destiny.
quotes.author: Mahatma Gandhi

quotes.text: The secret of change is to focus all of your energy not on fighting the old, but on building the new.
quotes.author: Socrates

quotes.text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: The mind, once stretched by a new idea, never returns to its original dimensions.
quotes.author: Oliver Wendell Holmes

quotes.text: Neurons that fire together wire together.
quotes.author: Donald Hebb

quotes.text: The brain is wider than the sky.
quotes.author: Emily Dickinson

quotes.text: Vulnerability is not weakness. It is our greatest measure of courage.
quotes.author: Brené Brown

quotes.text: You cannot swim for new horizons until you have courage to lose sight of the shore.
quotes.author: William Faulkner

quotes.text: The quality of your life is determined by the quality of your questions.
quotes.author: Tony Robbins

quotes.text: Happiness is not something ready-made. It comes from your own actions.
quotes.author: Dalai Lama

quotes.text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.
quotes.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

quotes.text: Feelings are just visitors. Let them come and go.
quotes.author: Mooji

quotes.text: The primary cause of unhappiness is never the situation but your thoughts about it.
quotes.author: Eckhart Tolle

quotes.text: Realize deeply that the present moment is all you have. Make the NOW the primary focus of your life.
quotes.author: Eckhart Tolle

quotes.text: Don't believe everything you think.
quotes.author: Byron Katie

quotes.text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow will be our doubts of today.
quotes.author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

quotes.text: Growth is painful. Change is painful. But nothing is as painful as staying stuck somewhere you don't belong.
quotes.author: Mandy Hale

quotes.text: The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek.
quotes.author: Joseph Campbell
quotes.source: The Hero with a Thousand Faces

quotes.text: Man cannot discover new oceans unless he has the courage to lose sight of the shore.
quotes.author: André Gide

quotes.text: What we achieve inwardly will change outer reality.
quotes.author: Plutarch

quotes.text: To understand everything is to forgive everything.
quotes.author: Buddha

quotes.text: One can have no smaller or greater mastery than mastery of oneself.
quotes.author: Leonardo da Vinci

quotes.text: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: If you correct your mind, the rest of your life will fall into place.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: The things you think about determine the quality of your mind.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself, in your way of thinking.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: It is not the strongest of the species that survives, nor the most intelligent, but the one most responsive to change.
quotes.author: Charles Darwin

quotes.text: The mind is its own place, and in itself can make a heaven of hell, a hell of heaven.
quotes.author: John Milton

quotes.text: Where there is no struggle, there is no strength.
quotes.author: Oprah Winfrey

quotes.text: You must be the change you wish to see in the world.
quotes.author: Mahatma Gandhi

quotes.text: Education is not the filling of a pail, but the lighting of a fire.
quotes.author: W.B. Yeats

quotes.text: The greatest weapon against stress is our ability to choose one thought over another.
quotes.author: William James

quotes.text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
quotes.author: William James

quotes.text: If you change the way you look at things, the things you look at change.
quotes.author: Wayne Dyer

quotes.text: The mind is not a vessel to be filled, but a fire to be kindled.
quotes.author: Plutarch

quotes.text: Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity.
quotes.author: Simone Weil

quotes.text: The quieter you become, the more you can hear.
quotes.author: Ram Dass

quotes.text: Almost everything will work again if you unplug it for a few minutes, including you.
quotes.author: Anne Lamott

quotes.text: The body benefits from movement, and the mind benefits from stillness.
quotes.author: Sakyong Mipham
quotes.source: Turning the Mind Into an Ally

quotes.text: Breath is the bridge which connects life to consciousness, which unites your body to your thoughts.
quotes.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

quotes.text: The greatest discovery of any generation is that a human being can alter his life by altering his attitude.
quotes.author: William James

quotes.text: We don't see things as they are, we see them as we are.
quotes.author: Anaïs Nin

quotes.text: The only journey is the one within.
quotes.author: Rainer Maria Rilke

quotes.text: To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

quotes.text: Life shrinks or expands in proportion to one's courage.
quotes.author: Anaïs Nin

quotes.text: Out of your vulnerabilities will come your strength.
quotes.author: Sigmund Freud

quotes.text: The only way out is through.
quotes.author: Robert Frost

quotes.text: Begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.
quotes.author: Seneca

quotes.text: True life is lived when tiny changes occur.
quotes.author: Leo Tolstoy

quotes.text: Muddy water is best cleared by leaving it alone.
quotes.author: Alan Watts

quotes.text: This being human is a guest house. Every morning a new arrival.
quotes.author: Rumi

quotes.text: I am not what happened to me, I am what I choose to become.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: No tree, it is said, can grow to heaven unless its roots reach down to hell.
quotes.author: Carl Jung

quotes.text: Ring the bells that still can ring. Forget your perfect offering. There is a crack in everything. That's how the light gets in.
quotes.author: Leonard Cohen

quotes.text: The only Zen you find on the tops of mountains is the Zen you bring up there.
quotes.author: Robert Pirsig
quotes.source: Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance

quotes.text: You are not a drop in the ocean. You are the entire ocean in a drop.
quotes.author: Rumi

quotes.text: When you realize nothing is lacking, the whole world belongs to you.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu

quotes.text: The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing, in so far as it stands ready against the accidental and the unforeseen.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: To think is easy. To act is hard. But the hardest thing in the world is to act in accordance with your thinking.
quotes.author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

quotes.text: Be patient toward all that is unsolved in your heart and try to love the questions themselves.
quotes.author: Rainer Maria Rilke

quotes.text: The purpose of life is not to be happy. It is to be useful, to be honorable, to be compassionate, to have it make some difference that you have lived and lived well.
quotes.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

categories.description: Ancient Roman and Greek philosophy on resilience, virtue, and inner peace
